meta.title: Lincoln & Civil War
meta.historical_context: The struggle for the soul of America.

abraham-lincoln.name: Abraham Lincoln
abraham-lincoln.description: Saved the Union, freed the slaves, and kept the country together during its darkest hour.

robert-e-lee.name: Robert E. Lee
robert-e-lee.description: A brilliant tactician who fought for the wrong cause.

ulysses-s-grant.name: Ulysses S. Grant
ulysses-s-grant.description: The man who won the war. Unconditional Surrender Grant.

frederick-douglass.name: Frederick Douglass
frederick-douglass.description: Escaped slave who became the most powerful voice for freedom.

lincoln-survives.title: Lincoln Lives 🎩❤️
lincoln-survives.description: Lincoln survives the assassination and leads Reconstruction.
lincoln-survives.present_day_status: Lincoln is remembered as the greatest American. Race relations are far ahead of our timeline. 🇺🇸✨

gentle-reconstruction.title: Malice Toward None
gentle-reconstruction.description: Lincoln manages to bring the South back without the bitterness of Radical Reconstruction.

peaceful-abolition.title: The Great Compromise 🕊️💰
peaceful-abolition.description: Slavery ends without a war via paid emancipation.
peaceful-abolition.present_day_status: The Civil War is a "What If" scenario in books. America is less militarized. 🕊️💵

buyout.title: The Buyout
buyout.description: The Federal government pays $400 per slave. The South accepts.

slow-progress.long_term: Segregation persists but without the blood feud

confederacy-wins.title: Southern Victory 🏴⚔️
confederacy-wins.present_day_status: The USA and CSA share a heavily fortified border. You need a passport to go to Atlanta. Slavery ended in the 1890s, but Apartheid lasted until 1990. 🇺🇸🏴

gettysburg-win.title: High Water Mark
gettysburg-win.description: Pickett's Charge succeeds. The Union army is broken.

ww1-allies.consequence: USA backs Germany, CSA backs Britain

election-1860.title: The Election of 1860
election-1860.text: You are Abraham Lincoln. The South threatens to secede if you win. Your advisors suggest a compromise.

hold-firm.consequence: You win, but the South secedes. War looms.

buyout-plan.text: Propose a massive buyout of all slaves.
buyout-plan.consequence: It is expensive, but cheaper than war. The Border States listen.

fort-sumter.title: Fort Sumter (1861)
fort-sumter.text: Major Anderson is trapped. The Confederates demand surrender. If you send ships, they will fire.

resupply.text: Send the fleet. Defend the flag.
resupply.consequence: The South fires the first shot. The Civil War begins.

surrender.text: Let them have the fort. Avoid blood.
surrender.consequence: Weakness invites aggression. The South secedes peacefully.

emancipation.title: The Proclamation (1862)
emancipation.text: The war is going badly. We need a moral cause. We need to free the slaves.

sign.consequence: The war becomes a crusade for freedom. Europe stays out.

delay.consequence: Britain recognizes the Confederacy. We lose the initiative.

gettysburg.title: Gettysburg (1863)
gettysburg.text: Lee has invaded the North. Everything depends on this battle. General Meade asks for orders.

hold-line.text: Defend the high ground at all costs.
hold-line.consequence: Pickett's Charge fails. The Union is saved.

retreat.consequence: Lee takes the heights. He marches on Washington.

ford-theater.title: Ford's Theatre (1865)
ford-theater.text: The war is won. Mary wants to see a play. You are very tired.

go.text: Go to the play. The people expect it.
go.consequence: Booth enters the box. Thus always to tyrants.

stay.consequence: Booth is caught waiting outside. You survive to rebuild the nation.
